data: {"step_number": 2, "code": "import sympy as sp\nimport numpy as np\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n\n# Solve the equation\nx = sp.symbols('x')\nequation = sp.Eq(x + 2, 5)\nsolution = sp.solve(equation, x)\nprint(f\"Solution for x: {solution[0]}\")\n\n# Plot the function\nx_vals = np.linspace(-5, 5, 400)\ny_vals = x_vals + 2\n\nplt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))\nplt.plot(x_vals, y_vals, label='$f(x) = x + 2$')\nplt.axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.5)\nplt.axvline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.5)\nplt.scatter([float(solution[0])], [5], color='red', zorder=5, label=f'Solution point ({solution[0]}, 5)')\nplt.title('Graph of $f(x) = x + 2$')\nplt.xlabel('x')\nplt.ylabel('f(x)')\nplt.legend()\nplt.grid(True)\nplt.show()"}
